comment.body:
I’m wondering how the contraction of government jobs, tech jobs, and whatever other jobs get outsourced to AI will impact the market. You’d think it would be deflationary. But then extremely inflationary once UBI starts kicking in. Once the government is forced to expand welfare that’s when things will start getting wacky.

I guess Elon is saying the singularity is soon upon us! lol

comment.body:
A deflationary cycle would be very brief I think. But can be vicious. I don’t think it would last more than 4-6 months if it did happen. They’ll just stimulate the economy

I don’t believe it is a coincidence Elon was given a deadline for July 4 of next year on DOGE. Can’t see the future but in the event of a hard landing, the number of days until then is oddly familiar

comment.body: Think GFC. If the market topped here in February, and we get a hard landing with a large decline. I’d take a bet it bottoms around July 4th 2026 lol

comment.body: I also don’t believe AI is slowing. And it wouldn’t in a hard landing. I believe a lot of AI companies will fail because their revenue is based off of curiosity rather than sustainability. But, those losses would be offset by the growth of AI companies who have customers for 6+ months. Those AI companies won’t lose customers even in a recession. If anything, they’ll gain

comment.body: I think the point being made is events don’t control the market, the market controls events.  Probably best to turn off tv and social media.  This is a small sample size and yet everyone is trying to figure out which “event” is the pivot and distracted from price and other indicators
comment.raw_body: I think the point being made is events don’t control the market, the market controls events.  Probably best to turn off tv and social media.  This is a small sample size and yet everyone is trying to figure out which “event” is the pivot and distracted from price and other indicators

comment.body: I don’t think anyone is distracted. The narratives “market leads the economy” or “market creates the economy” or “market controls events.” Doesn’t matter how you phrase it. The truth is its “market creates fraud and a false reality.” If that truth were recognized, then real change and prosperity could actually come. We could actually solve problems instead of what we got the past 4 years of stupidity

comment.body:
I have been trying to scroll back in the chat history for Alma’s take on nvda er. Could someone who knows the thread please point me to it?  I’m trying to digest her comments this afternoon about nvda and insiders lowering exposures (today AM) on supportive negative speed… prior to the flush that happened late in the day. She also mentions a BTD opportunity AFTER nvda er. So I gather she’s thinking further downside reaction to the ER and then a BTD opportunity post ER. 

Am I following correctly?

comment.body: Hi Alma, I was reviewing old post - the weekly analysis 02/18-02/21, still a lot to learn, this was from it - "This tells me that customers simply speculate on a natural event vol into Thursday as they assume, that the crowd still believes that trade war or inflation has anything to do with the market momentum, and they bet on the demand for left-tail hedges only to dump on them into Friday, as they bet on upside vol into Friday expo, and dump 5d put and 22d put vol. To realize upside vol, SPX should break above 6165 lvl, and this is what the market basically bets on." - However, as based on what happened, it was a flush for the entire day. It looks like the fixed delta IV graphic was telling a different story compared what would happen later. I might not understand correctly. Would you please explain a bit?

comment.body:
this is why we have intraday updates :) and i always say at the end, that we go day by day, bcs the positions can change... small prob events can happen.
i talked about friday suppressive flows on thrusday night, friday am.
and also note what i said in this weekly post about that flush.
i also signaled put on wednesday and thrusday too that bloomed green.
so 1+2 = the crash wasn't there as some expected.
